response.justification: James Smith's career is anchored in TypeScript/Node.js, D language, and web/mobile development, with Java appearing only in minor supporting roles (robotic arms, a brief NI IDE stint in C/C++). The role demands deep Java and Kotlin expertise, strong Spring ecosystem experience, and genuine IDE internals knowledge — none of which are present in a meaningful way. His tooling instincts and craftsmanship mindset are genuine positives, but they do not offset the fundamental mismatch in the required technical stack.
strengths: Demonstrated tooling-builder mindset: created test runners (Trial) and assertion libraries (Fluent-Asserts), showing genuine developer-experience focus
Brief IDE-adjacent experience at National Instruments writing C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI
Consistent software craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD) across multiple roles
concerns: Kotlin is entirely absent from the resume — a core technical requirement of the role
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise Java framework experience anywhere in 17+ years of history
Primary expertise is TypeScript and D language; Java appears only peripherally and never in a JVM-depth or enterprise context

key_factors.factor: Java expertise is peripheral and non-enterprise
